pages.full_text:
festival. Peter read to the children in the Maker Take tent. Charles + Jenny joined us. Simon + Peter went to a talk "War" by AC Grayling. Eve bought books + a puzzle for Conrad. Jenny + Charles, Conrad, Eleni + Eve just got back to the van before rain fell. We did the dragon puzzle + read. S + P returned. Some of us returned to the campsite. John + Jenny went to talks.
[illegible] In the evening Simon put Co K. to bed. Charles + Jenny spent the night in the van with them (pillows not comfy.) Peter took Eve back to Hay. He + John went to one lecture, while Eve went to "Vikings + Sagas" - brilliant - by Eleanor Barradough. Peter John + Eve had supper (veg terrine + duck) + Simon arrived. We walked by the river + at 11 pm went to St. Mary's church for a candlelit concert. We had to stand outside rather too long but were rewarded by impressive and sensitive cello playing interspersed with poetry. Jeanette Winterton was sitting in front of us.
pages.notes: Page is a continuation from a previous spread — no heading or date visible on this page. 'Hay' almost certainly refers to Hay-on-Wye (the literary festival context supports this). 'Co K.' likely an abbreviation for a child's name (Conrad/Kos or similar). 'Eleanor Barradough' may be 'Barraclough' — transcribed as written. 'Jeanette Winterton' is likely 'Jeanette Winterson' (the author) — transcribed as written.

pages.full_text:
__Sunday June 4th__
   We had spent the night in the barn which Peter had rented. Then we had a very crowded breakfast in the van where I burnt half the toast.
   Everyone else left + we cleared up + visited Abbey Dore (founded 1147) + had coffee, tea. We have forgotten the veg. knife, coffee pot, shaving brush, butter.
   Then home.

__Friday July 28th__        __Bromyard__
Eve drove red jazz to farm. Simon drove camping van + parked in farm yard. Saturday Hooray! Charles' + Jenny's wedding at Belmont Abbey. Reception at the top of the hill at the farm. Charles' van got a puncture at the Abbey. The trailer (for taking people up the hill) axel broke. A van got a flat tire. Cars skidded, collided with trees. The rain rained + rained. Mud mud mud. Absolutely glorious party.
pages.notes: Page number 112 clearly visible at top right. 'Sunday June 4th' and 'Friday July 28th' are both underlined as headings. 'Bromyard' is written above/beside 'Friday July 28th' as a location heading, also underlined. Year cannot be determined from this page alone — volume B covers 2003–2019; 'Sunday June 4' falls on a Sunday in 2006 and 2017; 'Friday July 28' falls on a Friday in 2006 and 2017. Both dates being consistent with the same year (2006) is possible but unconfirmed. Abbey Dore is in Herefordshire. Belmont Abbey is near Hereford. The wedding entry mentions 'Bromyard' which is also in Herefordshire. 'axel' likely 'axle'.
